input persona: A religious historian specializing in the study of medieval Christian monasticism, particularly the Cistercian order and its influence in Spain.
synthesized text:
(In the scriptorium of the Monastery of Santa María de Huerta, 13th century Spain...)

Brother Juan: "Brother Rodrigo, have you finished transcribing the latest batch of manuscripts from the abbey of Clairvaux?"

Brother Rodrigo: "Almost, Brother Juan. But I've come across an interesting passage in one of the letters from Saint Bernard. It speaks of the importance of manual labor in the pursuit of spiritual enlightenment."

Brother Juan: "Fascinating. I've been studying the Rule of Saint Benedict and its application in our own order. The emphasis on ora et labora is indeed crucial to our way of life."

(Just then, a novice monk bursts into the scriptorium, looking flustered.)

Novice Monk: "Brothers, forgive me, but I've just received a message from the abbey of Fitero. They've discovered a hidden chamber beneath their church, containing ancient relics and mysterious artifacts."

Brother Juan: "This is a remarkable find. We must send a delegation to Fitero to investigate and ensure the relics are properly venerated."

Brother Rodrigo: "I'll begin preparing a letter to the abbot of Fitero, requesting more information about the discovery."

(As the monks begin to discuss the implications of the discovery, a strange, unexplained glow begins to emanate from the manuscripts on the shelves, as if the words themselves are coming alive.)
label: instruction
